User.title: User
User.description: Represents a registered user of the MeetEase application, primarily for authentication and associating meeting requests.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The user's unique email address, used for login.

fullName.description: The full name of the user.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the user's information was updated.

AvailableSlot.title: AvailableSlot
AvailableSlot.description: Represents a specific time slot that can be booked for a meeting by a client.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AvailableSlot entity.

startTime.description: The start date and time of the available slot.

endTime.description: The end date and time of the available slot.

isBooked.description: A flag indicating whether the slot has been booked by a meeting. Default to false.

meetingId.description: Reference to the Meeting entity that has booked this slot, if applicable. (Relationship: AvailableSlot 1:1 Meeting)

Meeting.title: Meeting
Meeting.description: Represents a meeting request made by a client, including its details, status, and associated slot.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Meeting entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who scheduled this meeting. (Relationship: User 1:N Meeting)

clientName.description: The name of the client provided during the meeting scheduling.

clientMobile.description: The mobile number of the client provided during the meeting scheduling.

description.description: A detailed description of the meeting purpose, potentially generated by an AI tool.

paymentProofUrl.description: A URL pointing to the uploaded payment proof for the meeting.

status.description: The current status of the meeting (e.g., pending, confirmed, rejected, cancelled).

availableSlotId.description: Reference to the AvailableSlot entity booked for this meeting. (Relationship: Meeting 1:1 AvailableSlot)

meetingLink.description: The link for the actual online meeting (e.g., Zoom, Google Meet), provided once confirmed.

adminNotes.description: Internal notes added by an administrator regarding this meeting request.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the meeting request was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the meeting details or status were updated.

confirmationSentAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the confirmation email was sent to the client.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Each document ID matches the Firebase Authentication UID. Only the authenticated user matching 'userId' can read/write their own profile.

Stores globally available meeting time slots. Readable by all authenticated users. Writable only by administrators. Includes denormalized 'isBooked' and 'meetingId' fields which are updated atomically.

params.name: slotId
params.description: The unique identifier for an available time slot.

definition.description: Stores all meeting requests. Each document includes the 'userId' of the client who scheduled it for direct authorization. Users can only read/write their own meetings. Administrators can read/write all meetings. This structure enables QAPs for both users (filtering by 'userId') and admins (listing all). Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: meetingId
params.description: The unique identifier for a meeting request.

definition.description: Used for Database Access Control (DBAC). The existence of a document at this path signifies that the 'adminUid' has administrative privileges. Used to grant elevated permissions to admins.

params.name: adminUid
params.description: The unique identifier of a user who has administrative privileges.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes flat collections and denormalization to achieve robust security and scalability in the MeetEase application. This approach ensures Authorization Independence, simplifies security rules, and supports Query-as-Permission (QAP) patterns.

1.  **Users (`/users/{userId}`):** User profiles are stored in a top-level collection, with each document's ID matching the Firebase Authentication UID. This follows the private data access model, ensuring users can only read/write their own profiles. This collection naturally achieves Authorization Independence as `request.auth.uid` directly maps to the document ID.

2.  **Available Slots (`/availableSlots/{slotId}`):** All available meeting slots are stored in a global, top-level collection. This collection has a homogeneous security posture: read-only for all authenticated users (enabling QAPs for clients to fetch all available slots) and writeable only by administrators. The `isBooked` and `meetingId` fields are updated atomically when a meeting is created or an admin manually adjusts a slot. Authorization Independence is maintained as no parent document lookups are needed; rules check `request.auth.uid` against a global admin role collection for write access.

3.  **Meetings (`/meetings/{meetingId}`):** Meeting requests are stored in a single, top-level collection. Crucially, each `Meeting` document contains a denormalized `userId` field, linking it directly to the user who scheduled it. This is the cornerstone of Authorization Independence for meetings: security rules can determine ownership by checking `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` without performing any `get()` operations on parent documents. This structure perfectly supports QAPs:
    *   **For Clients (Meeting History):** Users can query `db.collection('meetings').where('userId', '==', currentUser.uid)`. The rules allow `list` operations only if the `userId` in the query matches `request.auth.uid`, making it secure and efficient.
    *   **For Admins (Dashboard):** Administrators (identified by the `/roles_admin` collection) can perform a `list` operation on the entire `/meetings` collection to retrieve all meeting requests for their dashboard. The rules will grant `list` permission based on their admin role without requiring any client-side filters, fulfilling the QAP for global admin access.

4.  **Admin Roles (`/roles_admin/{adminUid}`):** A dedicated collection is used for Database Access Control (DBAC). The mere existence of a document at `/roles_admin/{adminUid}` grants administrative privileges. This design uses 'Existence over Content' for global roles, making it simple and robust for rule evaluation (`exists(/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))`). This is inherently authorization-independent as it's a global check.
